items.caption: An educational diagram explaining the concept of isotope notation. it features a central box labeled "isotope notation" with a red "x" symbolizing the element. to the left of this box, there are two smaller boxes, one labeled "mass number" and the other "atomic number," both connected to the central box by arrows. the "mass number" box is colored blue, and the "atomic number" box is green. below the central box, there is a text that reads "ex. write carbon-12 and carbon-13 in isotope notation. determine the number of protons, electrons and neutrons." the text and the diagram together suggest a lesson on how to represent isotopes of elements using the isotope notation system.
